demos.diff:
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+from enum import Enum
+
+class ImageMode(str, Enum):
+    BINARY = "Binary (black & white)"
+    GRAYSCALE = "Grayscale"
+    UINT16 = "uint16"
+    COLOR = "Color"
+
+DEFAULT_COLORMAP = "Default"
\ No newline at end of file
gold_comments:
Not sure I like these values. I get that they mirror the choices of `image_mode` in `convertobjectstoimage` but I think they should be separated entirely. Right now we're manually duplicating code (if we change the value in `"convertobjectstoimage"` we'd need to change it here manually to keep them in sync) without even getting the advantage of redefining the string values to be something more sensible for an enum value.

The choices for `image_mode` in `ConvertObjectsToImage` should firstly be top level constants in `convertobjectstoimage`. The values of those constants are UI values, so should stay there.

The values for this `ImageMode` enum should mirror them, but need not use the exact same values, e.g. just `"binary"` instead of `"Binary (black & white)"`.

The choices for `image_mode` in `ConvertObjectsToImage` should firstly be top level constants in `convertobjectstoimage`. The values of those constants are UI values, so should stay there.

The values for this `ImageMode` enum should mirror them, but need not use the exact same values, e.g. just `"binary"` instead of `"Binary (black & white)"`.

gold_comments:
Ideally, we wouldn't have a single place in the frontend or core that imports or uses centrosome. Here it's being used for `display`, which means it doesn't belong in `library/modules` but having these three lines as a utility function in `library/functions/image_processing` and using that instead here would be preferred.  

Maybe something like (feel free to make prettier, or not):

```
stretched_rgb_from_components(r, g=None, b=None):
    if g:
        assert r.shape == g.shape
    if b:
        assert r.shape == b.shape

    color_image = numpy.zeros((r.shape[0], r.shape[1], 3))

    color_image[:, :, 0] = centrosome.filter.stretch(r)
    if g:
        color_image[:, :, 1] = centrosome.filter.stretch(g)
    if b:
        color_image[:, :, 2] = centrosome.filter.stretch(b)

    return color_image
```
`centrosome.filter` still needs to be removed from imports

issues.description:
Ideally, we wouldn't have a single place in the frontend or core that imports or uses centrosome. Here it's being used for `display`, which means it doesn't belong in `library/modules` but having these three lines as a utility function in `library/functions/image_processing` and using that instead here would be preferred.  

Maybe something like (feel free to make prettier, or not):

```
stretched_rgb_from_components(r, g=None, b=None):
    if g:
        assert r.shape == g.shape
    if b:
        assert r.shape == b.shape

    color_image = numpy.zeros((r.shape[0], r.shape[1], 3))

    color_image[:, :, 0] = centrosome.filter.stretch(r)
    if g:
        color_image[:, :, 1] = centrosome.filter.stretch(g)
    if b:
        color_image[:, :, 2] = centrosome.filter.stretch(b)

    return color_image
```
`centrosome.filter` still needs to be removed from imports

         #
         # Save the output image in the image set and have it inherit
         # mask & cropping from the original image.
gold_comments: We should have `fix_illumination_function` implement a custom Exception class, and then raise that. Here we check if it is of that class, rather than checking the text of the error message. That way if the errror message changes in `fix_illumination_function`, we don't have to remember to change it here as well.

signature.instructions:
Review a single-file diff the way a senior CellProfiler maintainer would.

Focus on: dead/unused code, project conventions (import order, TODO format,
logging over print, library opts usage), and broken abstractions
(raw values where enums exist, magic constants).

Only flag real, concrete issues - skip vague suggestions or praise.
Emit zero issues if the diff is clean.

fields.description: Path of the file being reviewed
Unified diff for this single file
Concrete review issues; empty list if none
